title: How to View Subitems on Connected Boards
excerpt: I have a board named ‘stage’ which I reference using a connected boards column on my main Board. This setup allows us to view each entry of all the stages I’ve added to the Stages Board. Currently this is working and I c…

posts.author: claire12 (Claire Yancey)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a board named ‘stage’ which I reference using a connected boards column on my main Board. This setup allows us to view each entry of all the stages I’ve added to the Stages Board. Currently this is working and I can see the items in the linked Stages board. However, the current configuration does not permit us to view or select the sub-items of a stage that is currently in progress.

For instance, if I’m working on the ‘Initiation’ stage, which has sub-stages like ‘introduction’, ‘send initial package’, and ‘initial package completion’, I can only see the ‘Initiation’ stage listed in the ‘stage’ column on the Main Board.

It would be beneficial if we could view and select the sub-stages of a stage to accurately track progress. Is there a straightforward way to display sub-stages on a popup card view of a connect column? Should I consider reorganizing our Stages Board if necessary?

posts.author: LKat (Laura Katterhenry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Claire.

Because each item and subitem are distinct in monday, you’d have to connect any subitem columns from your stage board into your main board to see the info about them. A dashboard may be helpful here if you’re trying to see multiple things in one place - could that be an option?
